name: Elbmesh Write Failing Tests
title: Write Failing Elbmesh Scenario Tests
summary: Create failing tests first for an Elbmesh implementation slice using Resource/Action/Event scenario shapes and boundary checks.
description: Use this task before production implementation when a planned Elbmesh slice needs executable tests or a precise test plan.

testCommand.description: Targeted command expected to fail before implementation.

outputs.title: Failing Elbmesh tests
outputs.description: Scenario, contract, or architecture-rule tests that fail for the intended reason before production implementation.

outputs.title: Untestable or deferred checks
outputs.description: Explicit notes for checks that cannot yet be automated.

validations.title: Confirm planned slice
criteria: The slice belongs to the active Elbmesh phase or has an explicit task card.
Production implementation has not started for behavior that lacks tests or an explicit test plan.

validations.title: Run targeted tests and capture expected failure
Review failing tests against Elbmesh boundaries
passCriteria: Tests use Resource, Action, Event, Reaction, External Operation, View, Query, Policy, journal, or execution trace terminology accurately.
Tests assert typed errors or structured journal records where available instead of brittle strings.
Tests fail because behavior is missing, not because of syntax errors or unrelated fixtures.
The test-writing step does not implement production behavior beyond minimal scaffolding.

blocks.markdown: Write failing tests for `{{ task.input.sliceName }}` from `{{ task.input.taskCard }}`.

items: Read the task card, glossary, relevant ADRs, and affected source/tests before editing.
Choose the smallest scenario shape that proves the intended behavior or boundary rule.
Write tests that express Given Events or journal state, When an Action/Reaction/Projection/Query runs, Then Events, Receipts, typed errors, journals, Views, or manifest findings are asserted.
Prefer typed ActionFailure codes, ManifestValidationError codes, schema versions, metadata, Receipts, and journal record variants over string-only assertions.
Do not change architecture decisions inside test fixtures; report mismatches to the planning workflow instead.
Run the targeted test command and confirm the failure is expected. If a test cannot be automated yet, document the exact gap.

criteria.text: The targeted test command fails before production implementation for the intended behavior or boundary gap.
Tests use Elbmesh Resource, Action, Event, Reaction, External Operation, View, Query, Policy, journal, manifest, or execution trace terminology consistently.
Test fixtures do not change architecture decisions or mix execution failures into Resource event streams.
No production behavior is implemented beyond minimal scaffolding needed for compiling the failing tests.
